projects.title: Lewis University Responsible AI Use Policy
projects.description: The university-wide framework governing ethical AI adoption, data privacy, academic integrity expectations, and responsible experimentation across all colleges and administrative units.
projects.contributor: Office of the Provost & AI Governance Committee

projects.title: AI-Augmented Curriculum Design Guidelines
projects.description: Institutional guidelines for integrating AI tools into course design, including approved tool lists, transparency requirements, and assessment adaptation protocols.

projects.title: College of Nursing AI Integration Policy
projects.description: Adapts the university AI policy for clinical education contexts, addressing AI use in patient simulation, HIPAA-adjacent data handling, and evidence-based practice research with generative tools.
projects.contributor: Dr. Maria Santos, College of Nursing

projects.title: College of Business Generative AI Standards
projects.description: Sets expectations for AI tool use in business coursework, case competitions, and capstone projects. Includes disclosure templates and rubric modifications for AI-assisted deliverables.

projects.title: CAST AI & Academic Integrity Framework
projects.description: The College of Aviation, Science & Technology's approach to AI in technical coursework: when AI-generated code is acceptable, how to cite AI contributions in lab reports, and simulation integrity standards.

projects.title: English Department AI Writing Policy
projects.description: A nuanced, assignment-level framework for AI use in composition and literature courses. Distinguishes between brainstorming, drafting, and revision stages, with different AI permissions at each phase.

projects.title: Criminal Justice Research Methods AI Protocol
projects.description: Department guidelines for using AI in qualitative coding, literature reviews, and data analysis within criminal justice research methods courses. Emphasizes algorithmic bias awareness.
projects.contributor: Dr. Omar Hassan, Criminal Justice Dept.

projects.title: Faculty AI Evaluation Toolkit
projects.description: A comprehensive decision-support tool with 1,131 activities across 161 rules, helping faculty select discipline-appropriate AI tools with Bloom's taxonomy alignment and full source provenance.

projects.title: Clinical Documentation AI Tutor
projects.description: An interactive simulation where nursing students practice writing SOAP notes with AI-generated patient scenarios. Provides real-time feedback on clinical language, completeness, and accuracy.

projects.title: Aviation NOTAM Decoder
projects.description: Translates dense FAA Notice to Air Missions (NOTAM) formatting into plain-language briefings. Built for aviation students learning to parse operational communications.

projects.title: Statistical Regression Lab
projects.description: An interactive environment where students build, visualize, and interpret regression models with AI-guided explanations. Adjustable datasets, real-time residual plots, and plain-language output.
projects.contributor: Dr. Lisa Chen, Mathematics & Dr. James Whitfield, Business

projects.title: Constitutional Law Case Analyzer
projects.description: Students input case citations and receive AI-generated briefs, holding summaries, and doctrinal lineage maps. Designed for pre-law and political science coursework.

projects.title: Human-Centered AI Writing Workshop
projects.description: A three-session lesson plan for English composition courses exploring AI as a writing collaborator. Students draft, critique AI output, and reflect on authorship, voice, and intellectual ownership.

projects.title: AI Ethics Across the Curriculum Module
projects.description: A drop-in teaching module adaptable to any discipline. Three 50-minute sessions covering bias in training data, labor implications of AI, and discipline-specific ethical scenarios. Includes case studies from nursing, business, aviation, and criminal justice.
projects.contributor: Dr. Sarah Voss, Philosophy & Dr. Marcus Bell, Theology

projects.title: AI-Assisted Data Journalism Project
projects.description: A semester-long project template where journalism and data science students collaborate to investigate local datasets using AI for pattern detection, visualization generation, and narrative drafting.
projects.contributor: Prof. Diana Ortiz, Journalism & Dr. Lisa Chen, Mathematics

projects.title: Biology Lab Safety Prompt Library
projects.description: A curated collection of prompts for generating lab safety protocols, hazard assessments, and emergency response procedures. Faculty-vetted for accuracy across BSL-1 and BSL-2 environments.

projects.title: Socratic Questioning Prompt Kit
projects.description: Discipline-agnostic prompts for using AI as a Socratic interlocutor in any classroom. Organized by Bloom's taxonomy level with examples from humanities, sciences, and professional programs.
projects.contributor: Dr. Sarah Voss, Philosophy & Center for Teaching & Learning Excellence

projects.title: AI-Assisted Assignment Rubric Template
projects.description: A modular rubric framework for evaluating student work that incorporates AI tools. Scores on prompt engineering quality, critical evaluation of AI output, synthesis with original analysis, and transparent documentation of AI contributions.

projects.title: Clinical Documentation AI Rubric
projects.description: Evaluates nursing students' ability to use AI tools appropriately in clinical documentation. Assesses accuracy, clinical judgment beyond AI suggestions, patient safety awareness, and HIPAA compliance.
projects.contributor: Dr. Maria Santos, College of Nursing

projects.title: Business Ethics AI Case Generator Guide
projects.description: A faculty guide for using AI to generate realistic business ethics case studies. Includes prompt templates, quality-control checklists, and a framework for ensuring cases reflect diverse stakeholder perspectives.
projects.contributor: Dr. James Whitfield, Business & Dr. Sarah Voss, Philosophy

projects.title: AI Literacy Competency Framework
projects.description: A scaffolded framework mapping AI literacy competencies to undergraduate learning outcomes. Defines what students should know about AI at the 100-, 200-, 300-, and 400-level, with discipline-specific extensions.

projects.title: AI Explainer Video Production Workflow
projects.description: A complete production pipeline for creating educational explainer videos using AI: script generation, visual storyboarding, voiceover synthesis, and editing. Includes prompt templates and quality checkpoints.

projects.title: AI-Augmented Literature Review Workflow
projects.description: A step-by-step workflow for using AI to accelerate literature reviews without compromising scholarly rigor. Covers source discovery, thematic coding, synthesis drafting, and citation verification.

projects.title: AI in Theological Reflection: A Student Guide
projects.description: A guide for theology students exploring how AI tools can assist (and complicate) theological reflection, exegesis, and pastoral writing. Engages questions of co-creation, authorship, and the sacred.
projects.scope: College of Humanities, Fine Arts & Communications

projects.title: Physics Simulation Companion
projects.description: AI-generated interactive physics simulations with adjustable parameters for kinematics, optics, and electromagnetism. Designed as a supplement to lecture demonstrations with built-in conceptual checkpoints.

projects.title: Psychology Research Methods AI Companion
projects.description: An AI-powered study companion that walks psychology students through research design, IRB considerations, statistical test selection, and APA-formatted results interpretation with interactive examples.
projects.contributor: Dr. Nina Alvarez, Psychology & Dr. Omar Hassan, Criminal Justice

projects.title: AI & Society Capstone Resource Hub
projects.description: A shared resource hub for the cross-listed AI & Society capstone course. Draws case studies, ethical frameworks, technical demos, and policy analyses from seven departments. A living example of what the Commons makes possible.
